# Team Capacity

Use this file as a planning constraint, not a utilization target.

## Team mode example
- Sprint length: 2 weeks
- Team members: 4
- Nominal delivery capacity: 30 story points
- Reserve for unplanned work and interrupts: 20%
- Effective usable capacity: 24 story points
- Known constraints: one reviewer available only part-time; QA support available mainly in week two

## Solo mode example
- Sprint length: 1 week
- Team members: 1
- Nominal delivery capacity: 10 story points
- Reserve for unplanned work and context switching: 20%
- Effective usable capacity: 8 story points
- Known constraints: one person is covering product thinking, planning, execution, and validation

## Rules
- Do not exceed effective usable capacity.
- Preserve reserve even when additional items appear attractive.
- Prefer a coherent sprint slice over maximizing point count.
- In solo mode, account for context switching as real delivery cost.
- In team mode, account for handoffs, review bottlenecks, and sequencing constraints.
